snp2.12:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Āḷavī at the Aggāḷava shrine.
snp2.12:1.3:
And on that occasion Ven. Vaṅgīsa’s preceptor, an elder named Nigrodha Kappa,
had recently totally unbound at the Aggāḷava shrine.
snp2.12:1.4: Then as Ven. Vaṅgīsa was alone in seclusion, this train of thought arose in his awareness:
snp2.12:1.5: “Has my preceptor totally unbound, or has he not totally unbound?”
snp2.12:1.6:
Then, emerging from his seclusion in the late afternoon, Ven.
Vaṅgīsa went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
As he was sitting there, he said to the Blessed One,
snp2.12:1.7: “Just now, as I was alone in seclusion, this train of thought arose in my awareness:
snp2.12:1.8: ‘Has my preceptor totally unbound, or has he not totally unbound?’”
snp2.12:1.9:
Then, arranging his robe over one shoulder and placing his hands palm-to-palm over his heart toward the Blessed One,
Ven. Vaṅgīsa addressed the Blessed One in verses:
snp2.12:2.1: “We ask the Teacher of supreme discernment,
snp2.12:2.2: who has cut off uncertainty in the here-&-now:
snp2.12:2.3: A monk has died at the Aggāḷava shrine—
snp2.12:2.4: well-known, prestigious, with mind fully unbound.
snp2.12:3.2: given by you, Blessed One, to that brahman.
snp2.12:3.4: who sees the firm Dhamma— intent on release, with persistence aroused.
snp2.12:4.2: we all, too, want to know of that disciple:
snp2.12:4.3: Ready to hear are our ears.
snp2.12:4.4:
You, our teacher:
You are unexcelled.
snp2.12:5.1:
Cut through our uncertainty.
Tell me this,
snp2.12:5.2: make known, One of discernment deep, that he was totally unbound.
snp2.12:5.3: Like Thousand-eyed Sakka, in the midst of the devas,
snp2.12:6.1: Here, whatever snares there are, paths of delusion, siding
snp2.12:6.3: On reaching the Tathāgata, they don’t exist,
snp2.12:6.4: as that Eye is the foremost of men.
snp2.12:7.1: For if no man were ever to disperse defilements—
snp2.12:7.2: as the wind, a dark mass of clouds—
snp2.12:7.3: the whole world would be enveloped in darkness.
snp2.12:7.4: Even brilliant people wouldn’t shine bright.
snp2.12:8.1: But the enlightened are makers of light.
snp2.12:8.2: Thus I think you’re that, enlightened one.
snp2.12:8.3: We have come to one who knows through clear-seeing.
snp2.12:8.4: Make Kappa shine in our assembly.
snp2.12:9.1: Quickly, handsome one, stir your handsome voice.
snp2.12:9.2: Like a swan, stretching out (its neck), call gently
snp2.12:9.4: We all listen to you, sitting upright.
snp2.12:10.1: Pleading, I shall get the pure one to speak,
snp2.12:10.2: he whose birth & death are abandoned.
snp2.12:10.3: For people run-of-the-mill haven’t the power to bring about what they desire,
snp2.12:10.4: but Tathāgatas do have the power to bring about what they have pondered.
snp2.12:11.2: is rightly-grasped, you of discernment rightly straight.
snp2.12:11.4: Knowing, don’t delude us, one of discernment supreme.
snp2.12:12.1: Understanding the noble Dhamma from high to low,
snp2.12:12.2: knowing, don’t delude us, hero supreme.
snp2.12:12.3: I long for the water of your speech as if distressed in mind by the heat in the summer.
snp2.12:13.1: Was the holy life, as led by Kappa in line with his aim?
snp2.12:13.2: Was it not in any way in vain?
snp2.12:13.3: Did he unbind with no fuel remaining?
snp2.12:13.4: Let us hear how he was released.”
snp2.12:14.1: “Here he cut off craving for name-&-form,
snp2.12:14.3: the current of the Dark One, the long-time obsession.
snp2.12:14.4: He has crossed over birth & death.”
snp2.12:14.5: So spoke the Blessed One, excelling in five.
snp2.12:15.2: highest of seers, I am brightened & calmed.
snp2.12:15.3: Surely, my question was not in vain,
snp2.12:15.4: nor was I deceived by the brahman.
snp2.12:16.1: As he spoke, so he acted:
snp2.12:16.2: He was a disciple of the One Awakened.
snp2.12:16.4: the tough, stretched-out net of deceitful Death.
snp2.12:17.4: the realm of Death so very hard to cross.”
